title: 入黎睇下外媒點睇今次選舉結果

replies.msg:
https://www.facebook.com/goodbyehkhellouk/posts/681491075712699
"說好了的「沉默大多數」响呢個被國際輿論稱之為對「暴徒」咁多個月嘅「所作所為」"de facto referendum”（實際上嘅變相公投），香港人比咗一個大比數嘅答案。基本上無一個國際媒體認為係整個運動嘅完結，大部份都係覺得只係另一個開始。"

《BBC》記者Stephen McDonell評論：
"Nobody imagined such a comprehensive wipeout, and Carrie Lam's administration will no doubt come under renewed pressure to listen to the demands of protestors following such an overwhelming defeat for her and her allies."
（無人能夠想象到出現橫掃得咁徹底，依家阿Carrie個政府同佢啲黨羽肯定要面對更大嘅壓力，去回應示威者嘅訴求。）

《The Telegraph》訪問嚟香港監察嘅Lord Alton
// “If great cities like Paris or London or New York can have directly elected mayors then why can’t Hong Kong? The answer to that is that Beijing has opposed it.”//
（「如果巴黎同倫敦都可以直選市長，點解香港唔得？答案只係北京反對之嗎。」）

《Financial Times》：
// “We can’t be on the front lines but I hope to try to help our young people by casting a vote,” said Raymond Ng, a surveyor in his early 40s who brought his 65-year-old mother to vote for the first time.//
（「我地唔可以上前線，但係希望用選票幫下啲年青人。」）

《The Guardian》：
// Stephen, a retired businessman in his 60s voting in the affluent Mid-Levels neighbourhood, said: “This will send the message to the government that they should be more humble. It’s your job to serve people, and not beat people up if they don’t listen to you.”//
（今次就係比個訊息個政府，係要謙卑啲。你地份工係服務市民，唔係人地反對就出手打人。）

《New York Times》
// The performance of the pro-Beijing parties on Sunday suggests their support for the unpopular extradition bill hurt their image with voters. It also shows that their ability to galvanize voters has limits.（班友穿越選民底線嘅能力，係有極限㗎！）//

《The Washington Post》
// But this time around, elections that have typically been fought on issues such as traffic, trash collection and the nuisance of pests such as wild boars became a referendum on the most fundamental issue in the territory: Whether one stands with the movement fighting for democratic freedoms, or with the pro-Beijing establishment that has had a grip on the former colony since Britain handed it back to China in 1997.//
（本來係關乎交通、收垃圾、鼠王同野豬之類嘅選舉，已經變成香港核心價值嘅公投：到底撐爭取民主自由嘅抗爭，還是企响建制嗰邊，幫手將香港由1997年開始越鏈越緊？）

《Al Jazeera》：
//"These few months have really shown a new picture for what Hong Kong people can do. Can we also fight in those different fields? If we can, I think Hong Kong has a future." said Iverson Ha, 23, a university student studying politics.//
（「呢幾個月証明咗香港人嘅能力，如果問再可唔可以打更多戰線的話？做到的話，香港就有未來。」）

所以今次只係個開頭，我地要乘住呢股反擊之風，從體制內外一齊繼續抗爭

光復香港，時代革命
香港人，反抗

quote.msg:
https://www.facebook.com/goodbyehkhellouk/posts/681491075712699
"說好了的「沉默大多數」响呢個被國際輿論稱之為對「暴徒」咁多個月嘅「所作所為」"de facto referendum”（實際上嘅變相公投），香港人比咗一個大比數嘅答案。基本上無一個國際媒體認為係整個運動嘅完結，大部份都係覺得只係另一個開始。"

《BBC》記者Stephen McDonell評論：
"Nobody imagined such a comprehensive wipeout, and Carrie Lam's administration will no doubt come under renewed pressure to listen to the demands of protestors following such an overwhelming defeat for her and her allies."
（無人能夠想象到出現橫掃得咁徹底，依家阿Carrie個政府同佢啲黨羽肯定要面對更大嘅壓力，去回應示威者嘅訴求。）

《The Telegraph》訪問嚟香港監察嘅Lord Alton
// “If great cities like Paris or London or New York can have directly elected mayors then why can’t Hong Kong? The answer to that is that Beijing has opposed it.”//
（「如果巴黎同倫敦都可以直選市長，點解香港唔得？答案只係北京反對之嗎。」）

《Financial Times》：
// “We can’t be on the front lines but I hope to try to help our young people by casting a vote,” said Raymond Ng, a surveyor in his early 40s who brought his 65-year-old mother to vote for the first time.//
（「我地唔可以上前線，但係希望用選票幫下啲年青人。」）

《The Guardian》：
// Stephen, a retired businessman in his 60s voting in the affluent Mid-Levels neighbourhood, said: “This will send the message to the government that they should be more humble. It’s your job to serve people, and not beat people up if they don’t listen to you.”//
（今次就係比個訊息個政府，係要謙卑啲。你地份工係服務市民，唔係人地反對就出手打人。）

《New York Times》
// The performance of the pro-Beijing parties on Sunday suggests their support for the unpopular extradition bill hurt their image with voters. It also shows that their ability to galvanize voters has limits.（班友穿越選民底線嘅能力，係有極限㗎！）//

《The Washington Post》
// But this time around, elections that have typically been fought on issues such as traffic, trash collection and the nuisance of pests such as wild boars became a referendum on the most fundamental issue in the territory: Whether one stands with the movement fighting for democratic freedoms, or with the pro-Beijing establishment that has had a grip on the former colony since Britain handed it back to China in 1997.//
（本來係關乎交通、收垃圾、鼠王同野豬之類嘅選舉，已經變成香港核心價值嘅公投：到底撐爭取民主自由嘅抗爭，還是企响建制嗰邊，幫手將香港由1997年開始越鏈越緊？）

《Al Jazeera》：
//"These few months have really shown a new picture for what Hong Kong people can do. Can we also fight in those different fields? If we can, I think Hong Kong has a future." said Iverson Ha, 23, a university student studying politics.//
（「呢幾個月証明咗香港人嘅能力，如果問再可唔可以打更多戰線的話？做到的話，香港就有未來。」）

所以今次只係個開頭，我地要乘住呢股反擊之風，從體制內外一齊繼續抗爭

光復香港，時代革命
香港人，反抗

quote.msg:
https://www.facebook.com/goodbyehkhellouk/posts/681491075712699
"說好了的「沉默大多數」响呢個被國際輿論稱之為對「暴徒」咁多個月嘅「所作所為」"de facto referendum”（實際上嘅變相公投），香港人比咗一個大比數嘅答案。基本上無一個國際媒體認為係整個運動嘅完結，大部份都係覺得只係另一個開始。"

《BBC》記者Stephen McDonell評論：
"Nobody imagined such a comprehensive wipeout, and Carrie Lam's administration will no doubt come under renewed pressure to listen to the demands of protestors following such an overwhelming defeat for her and her allies."
（無人能夠想象到出現橫掃得咁徹底，依家阿Carrie個政府同佢啲黨羽肯定要面對更大嘅壓力，去回應示威者嘅訴求。）

《The Telegraph》訪問嚟香港監察嘅Lord Alton
// “If great cities like Paris or London or New York can have directly elected mayors then why can’t Hong Kong? The answer to that is that Beijing has opposed it.”//
（「如果巴黎同倫敦都可以直選市長，點解香港唔得？答案只係北京反對之嗎。」）

《Financial Times》：
// “We can’t be on the front lines but I hope to try to help our young people by casting a vote,” said Raymond Ng, a surveyor in his early 40s who brought his 65-year-old mother to vote for the first time.//
（「我地唔可以上前線，但係希望用選票幫下啲年青人。」）

《The Guardian》：
// Stephen, a retired businessman in his 60s voting in the affluent Mid-Levels neighbourhood, said: “This will send the message to the government that they should be more humble. It’s your job to serve people, and not beat people up if they don’t listen to you.”//
（今次就係比個訊息個政府，係要謙卑啲。你地份工係服務市民，唔係人地反對就出手打人。）

《New York Times》
// The performance of the pro-Beijing parties on Sunday suggests their support for the unpopular extradition bill hurt their image with voters. It also shows that their ability to galvanize voters has limits.（班友穿越選民底線嘅能力，係有極限㗎！）//

《The Washington Post》
// But this time around, elections that have typically been fought on issues such as traffic, trash collection and the nuisance of pests such as wild boars became a referendum on the most fundamental issue in the territory: Whether one stands with the movement fighting for democratic freedoms, or with the pro-Beijing establishment that has had a grip on the former colony since Britain handed it back to China in 1997.//
（本來係關乎交通、收垃圾、鼠王同野豬之類嘅選舉，已經變成香港核心價值嘅公投：到底撐爭取民主自由嘅抗爭，還是企响建制嗰邊，幫手將香港由1997年開始越鏈越緊？）

《Al Jazeera》：
//"These few months have really shown a new picture for what Hong Kong people can do. Can we also fight in those different fields? If we can, I think Hong Kong has a future." said Iverson Ha, 23, a university student studying politics.//
（「呢幾個月証明咗香港人嘅能力，如果問再可唔可以打更多戰線的話？做到的話，香港就有未來。」）

所以今次只係個開頭，我地要乘住呢股反擊之風，從體制內外一齊繼續抗爭

光復香港，時代革命
香港人，反抗
